content.problemStatement:
A sequence BBB is called good if Bimod‚ÄČ‚ÄČ2‚Č†Bi‚ąí1mod‚ÄČ‚ÄČ2B_i \mod 2 \ne B_{i - 1} \mod 2Bi‚Äčmod2ÓÄ†=Bi‚ąí1‚Äčmod2 for all 2‚Č§i‚Č§‚ą£B‚ą£2 \le i \le |B|2‚Č§i‚Č§‚ą£B‚ą£. For example, [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] and [4][4][4] are good sequences, but [1,3][1, 3][1,3] is not.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Find the length of the largest subsequence‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† of AAA that is good.

‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† A subsequence is a sequence that can be formed by deleting some (possibly zero) elements from the array without changing the order. For example, [1,3],[2][1, 3], [2][1,3],[2] and [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] are subsequences of [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] but [3,1][3, 1][3,1] is not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the length of the largest good subsequence of AAA.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We can choose the subsequence [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] which is good.
Test Case 2 : The entire sequence itself is good.
